contents:
# III MEF Information Group Announces Exercise Kaiju Rain 26 Start in Japan

## Overview
III Marine Expeditionary Force Information Group announced Exercise Kaiju Rain 26, set for May 1 to May 31, 2026, in Japan. The annual exercise involves about 1,000 Marines and Sailors training on communications, intelligence, and decision-making for Indo-Pacific operations. It includes cyber defense, electronic warfare, and live-fire events across dispersed environments.

## Details
Units from III MIG will conduct training in communications setup, small unmanned aircraft systems operations, logistics movements, resilient communications, and maritime sensing. The exercise aims to strengthen operations in austere settings. Announcement made by 1st Lt. Joshua Wolek on April 29. No specific JSDF units mentioned in announcement, but focuses on U.S. forces in Japan. No casualties or equipment losses noted. Integrates with broader US Forces Japan activities.[2]

**Location**: Multiple locations, Japan

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @USForcesJapan, which covers US Forces Japan deployments JSDF joint exercises. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following US Forces Japan deployments JSDF joint exercises will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
